bedtime_stories.The Sleepy Star: Once upon a time, a little star lived high up in the sky. Every night, it would shine as brightly as it could, lighting up the darkness for all the children below. But one night, the star felt very tired. It had been shining for so many hours! It decided to close its eyes, just for a moment. As it drifted off, it dreamed of faraway galaxies and dancing comets. When it woke up, the sun was rising, and it was time to rest for real. The star smiled, knowing it would shine again tonight. Sleep well, little star. Sleep well.
bedtime_stories.The Cozy Cloud: High above the hills, there lived a small, fluffy cloud named Nimbus. Nimbus loved floating gently through the sky, watching the world below. One evening, as the sun set in shades of pink and gold, Nimbus felt his eyes growing heavy. He drifted lower and lower, past the tallest mountains, past the tallest trees, until he wrapped himself gently around a hilltop like a soft blanket. The flowers below breathed in his cool mist and smiled. By morning, Nimbus had turned into the gentlest rain, nourishing every seed and flower. He had given the world a cozy gift in his sleep.
bedtime_stories.The Moon's Lullaby: The moon had a secret. Every night, when all the world was quiet, she would hum a soft lullaby. The stars would lean in close to listen, and one by one they would blink their eyes and grow still. The owls would tuck their heads beneath their wings. The foxes would curl their tails around their noses. Even the restless wind would settle into a gentle breeze. And somewhere below, a child just like you would hear that faint, sweet hum through the window, and slowly, gently, drift off into the most wonderful dreams.
bedtime_stories.The Little Lighthouse: On a rocky shore, a little lighthouse stood all alone. Each night it would spin its golden beam out across the dark water, guiding boats safely home. It was hard work, and some nights the lighthouse felt lonely. But then the ships would pass, and the sailors would wave, and the lighthouse would glow a little brighter. One calm night, after all the boats had found their harbors, the lighthouse sat quietly listening to the waves. The sea sang it a soft song, and for the first time in a long while, the little lighthouse rested — still glowing softly, but peacefully, in the quiet dark.
bedtime_stories.The Dreaming Fox: A young fox named Ember lived at the edge of a forest. Every evening, when the fireflies lit up the meadow, Ember would find a mossy spot beneath an old oak tree and watch the stars appear one by one. She would try to count them, but always fell asleep before she reached ten. In her dreams, she ran through golden fields, leaped over silver streams, and danced with the fireflies themselves. When morning came, she always woke up smiling, ready for a new day — but always a little sad to leave the dream behind. Tonight, Ember is counting the stars again. Maybe you can count with her.
